3.explanation:

The path includes explicit close calls for both readerBuffered and readerInputStream inside the same try block (lines 15–37), but these closes are not in a finally block. If an exception occurs before those close attempts (e.g., at line 10 or 12), the close attempts will be skipped, and the resources will not be closed. Since the marker branch already executed the source, and there is no guaranteed cleanup (no finally block ensuring closure on all continuations), the resource may remain unclosed on this path.

Answer: Yes
